description_long: This structure is a wooden-framed cowshed with an open framework, revealing vertical support beams and a pitched gable roof. The roof is constructed with evenly spaced rafters, leaving the interior exposed and allowing for ample ventilation. The floor appears to be a solid, dark surface, likely intended for durability and easy cleaning. There are no visible doors or windows, suggesting this is an unfinished or partially constructed agricultural building. The open sides and roof design are typical for livestock shelters, providing protection from rain while allowing airflow. The overall appearance is utilitarian, prioritizing function and structural simplicity.
description: A wooden-framed cowshed with an open gable roof and exposed vertical beams, designed for ventilation and livestock shelter.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the cowshed from a corner angle, highlighting the open gable roof structure and the spacious, unobstructed interior.
description_view_1: View 1 presents a direct side view, emphasizing the regular spacing of the vertical support beams and the open framework.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the opposite corner, providing a clear look at the roof's ridge and the symmetry of the frame.
description_view_3: View 3 offers a straight-on view of the other side, showing the full height of the vertical beams and the open, accessible interior.
attribute_ventilation_features: open eaves and open framework for natural ventilation
